commit.message:
fix(render): 修复嵌套 overlay 退化并统一 block 几何 (#18)

## Why
- `worker-configuration.d.ts` 这类大型 TypeScript 声明文件会在嵌套 overlay
合并阶段出现严重性能退化，看起来像卡死。
- 前一轮 render 优化暴露了两个视觉问题：多行注释跨行样式会丢失，嵌套 block 的几何又会被宿主包装行放大，导致 raw
string / JSDoc 这类区域的 block tint 范围不稳定。
- 日常调试时还需要显式写 `cargo run --bin kat`，命令有些啰嗦。

## What
- 把 `overlay_nested_region` 和 `overlay_style_spans` 改成基于已排序 spans 的线性
merge，避免对每个 injected region 全量重建整份 spans。
- 修正 `render_styled_spans` 的按行 span 切片逻辑，保留跨多行 span 在后续行上的活跃状态。
- 把嵌套注入区域的视觉策略收口到 runtime 统一推导：独立的多行 block 会自动拿到 block tint，显式 inline
注入继续保持透明叠加。
- 在 block 几何推导里区分 `content` / `blank` / `wrapper` 行；wrapper 行不再参与 block
的纵向或横向范围，宿主定界符也不会再被 block tint 包进去。
- 保留轻量版 `KAT_DEBUG_PROGRESS` 阶段日志，并把 `Cargo.toml` 的默认运行目标设成 `kat`。
- 补充回归测试，覆盖 overlay 合并语义、多行注释跨行样式、JSDoc block tint、inline template 注入保持
inline，以及 Rust raw-string SQL 的 block 几何。

## Testing
- `KAT_AGENT_TEST_LOG_MODE=quiet just test`
- `cargo run --release --
/Users/dcjanus/Code/hacknews-feeds/worker-configuration.d.ts --paging
never --debug-timing`
- `cargo run --release -- testdata/showcase/rust/injections.rs --paging
never --debug-timing`

---------

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.name: kat
repository.description: A syntax-highlighting cat built around Tree-sitter.

commit.message:
feat: model-aware max_tokens, translatable tool descriptions, ZAI tool calling, UI polish (#45)

* feat: model-aware max_tokens, translatable tool descriptions, ZAI tool calling, and UI polish

- Add model-aware max_tokens via GetModelMaxOutputTokens()/EffectiveMaxTokens
  on BaseAiProvider, with ZAI capped at 4095 (coding API limit)
- Gate request.Tools on ToolOutputFormat — providers using AireText no longer
  send native tools in the API request
- Fix ZAI/GLM tool calls: override DefaultToolOutputFormat to AireText and
  add unclosed <tool_call tag recovery in ToolCallParser
- Make all tool descriptions translatable with question format (e.g. "Take a
  screenshot?" instead of "Taking a screenshot") across all 12 languages
- Fix close button content color: change ✕ (U+2715) to × (U+00D7) and remove
  unnecessary AccentTextBrush Foreground overrides across 10 XAML files
- Add AccentTextBrush to AppTheme.xaml for title text consistency
- Refactor provider tests to share SimpleJsonServer infrastructure
- Add app-layer service tests (ChatInteraction, Conversation, ProviderCatalog)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: update tool description assertions for question format

BuildDescription() now produces question-style descriptions and recovers
unclosed tool_call tags, so tests were updated to match.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: human-readable provider errors + test coverage for 15 application services

- Replace generic catch-all error messages with descriptive ones in
  OpenAiProvider, GoogleAiProvider, and OllamaProvider
- Add authentication-specific errors, model-not-found errors, and
  network-error sanitization via ProviderErrorClassifier.SanitizeNetworkError()
- Stop leaking raw API response bodies in GoogleAiProvider and OllamaProvider
- Add streaming-failure error markers instead of silent yield break
- Add ~150 new tests covering 15 previously untested application services:
  MCP (McpCatalog, McpConfig, McpStartup), Onboarding (Ollama, ProviderSetup),
  Provider workflows (Setup, EditorSave, ModelCatalog, CapabilityTestSession,
  CodexAction, OllamaAction, OllamaModelCatalog), Tools (ApprovalPrompt,
  ApprovalExecution), Chat (ChatTurn), Settings (AppSettings),
  Connections (EmailAccount)
- Update roadmap with completed P0 tasks and progress on P2 items

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ci): exclude WPF MainWindowTests from default test run

MainWindowTests require an interactive desktop session (STA threading,
WPF window creation) which is unavailable in headless CI runners.
Exclude them from the default 'all' scope to prevent testhost crashes.
They can still be run explicitly with scope=ui.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
`make test-revise-*`: Initialize Revise fully on workers (#61321)

Set `JULIA_REVISE_WORKER_ONLY` before loading `Revise` on `Distributed`
workers in the revise test harness. This ensures worker-side Revise
initialization assigns the Julia source paths before `revise_trackall()`
calls `Revise.track` on `Base`, `Core.Compiler`, and stdlibs.

This fixes timholy/Revise.jl#1023.

🤖 Co-authored-by: Codex

commit.message:
Add npm test job to CI check workflow

Co-Authored-By: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(ci): 避免 release 构建产生 dirty 版本元信息 (#17)

## Why
- 当前 release CI 会对 `.github/scripts/rust-link-wrapper.sh` 执行 `chmod
+x`，导致构建时 Git worktree 变 dirty。
- `shadow-rs` 会把这个 dirty 状态写进 `kat --version`，影响通过 `cargo binstall`
安装下来的预编译二进制。

## What
- 删除 CI 里构建前对 linker wrapper 的额外 `chmod +x` 步骤。
- 直接把 `.github/scripts/rust-link-wrapper.sh` 以可执行权限纳入仓库，避免 release
构建修改工作树。

## Testing
- 未跑自动化测试；本次只调整 Git 文件模式和 workflow 步骤。
- 已本地确认变更仅包含 workflow 删除 `chmod` 与脚本权限位改为 `100755`。

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Honor hidden options in duplicate checks

Hidden options still participate in parsing, so constructor-time
duplicate option validation must treat them as syntax-bearing terms
instead of filtering them out as purely user-facing metadata.

Add regression tests for hidden-visible and hidden-hidden collisions in
object(), tuple(), and merge(), route duplicate detection through an
internal helper that includes fully hidden option names, and update the
docs and changelog to describe the construction-time behavior.

Fixes https://github.com/dahlia/optique/issues/510

Co-Authored-By: Codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.name: optique
repository.description: Type-safe combinatorial CLI parser for TypeScript

commit.message:
refactor(chat): remove lingering frontend budget suppressions (#674)

Summary:
- extract Project AI terminal connection logic into dedicated helpers and
  shared types so terminal lifecycle behavior no longer needs inline
  eslint budget suppressions
- drop the stale max-lines suppression from the workspace browser detail
  panel and flatten chat/settings tests that only used describe-level
  bypasses
- keep the cleanup scoped to the existing chat workspace and provider
  settings behavior while preserving current coverage

Rationale:
- ASE-172 asks for temporary frontend file-length bypasses to be replaced
  with maintainable implementations rather than more allowlists
- moving the websocket/session lifecycle into focused helpers makes the
  terminal manager easier to evolve without hiding debt behind disables
- removing stale suppressions keeps the structure rules honest and lets
  remaining warnings surface naturally instead of being silently ignored

Tests:
- cd web && PATH=$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.1/bin:$PATH corepack pnpm exec vitest run src/lib/features/chat/terminal-manager.test.ts src/lib/features/chat/project-conversation-workspace-browser-detail.test.ts src/lib/features/settings/components/agent-settings.test.ts
- cd web && PATH=$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.1/bin:$PATH corepack pnpm run lint:structure
- cd web && PATH=$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.1/bin:$PATH corepack pnpm run lint:deps
- cd web && PATH=$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.1/bin:$PATH corepack pnpm run check
- cd web && PATH=$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.1/bin:$PATH corepack pnpm run build
- PATH=$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.1/bin:$PATH make frontend-api-audit-check
- cd web && PATH=$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.1/bin:$PATH corepack pnpm run ci

Co-authored-by: betterandbetterii <betterandbetterii.user@github.com>
Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.name: openase
repository.description: Ticket-Driven Automated Software Engineering. OpenASE is an all-in-one platform that turns tickets into working code — AI agents automatically pick up tickets, execute workflows on your machines, and deliver results with full traceability.

commit.message:
Restore the Florida GeoJSON GPU path and add IO shootouts

Fix the large-file GeoJSON GPU path so public Florida reads no longer OOM
and silently fall back to CPU, tighten GeoDataFrame/native boundary
replacement overhead, and add reusable Florida corpus export plus per-format
vsbench shootout scripts for native IO comparisons.

Co-Authored-By: Codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.name: vibeSpatial
repository.description: GPU-first spatial analytics for Python. Drop-in GeoPandas replacement powered by runtime-compiled CUDA kernels

commit.message:
fix(slack): guide participant mentions through prompt context (#177)

Guide Slack mentions for known thread participants through prompt
context instead of rewriting outbound message text.

The earlier branch direction added a post-send mention resolver plus
workspace-wide Slack user lookups. That cut across the normal streaming
reply path, risked rewriting non-mention text like links and package
names, and added avoidable Slack API load. This version removes that
machinery and keeps the change at the existing identity-context
boundary.

We now inject thread participants into the system prompt with the exact
`<@USERID>` token the model should use when referring to someone already
in the conversation. Slack output formatting stays synchronous and
unchanged, which preserves the repo's standard streamed
`AsyncIterable<string>` reply path.

Integration coverage now protects the important behavior boundary by
asserting streamed Slack output is posted unchanged, while the unit
coverage stays focused on local output normalization.

Closes #174
Closes #175

---------

Co-authored-by: junior <junior@sentry.io>
Co-authored-by: David Cramer <dcramer@gmail.com>
Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Batch search submissions to reduce memory

Process queries in fixed-size batches, forcing a garbage collection cycle after each batch while preserving overall tqdm progress across the full query set.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.name: cheat-at-search
repository.description: Cheat at search with LLMs course materials

commit.message:
Implement live announcer core and DOM bootstrap (#514)

* Implement live announcer core and DOM bootstrap

Add the ars-a11y LiveAnnouncer core, ars-dom live-region bootstrap, coverage-focused announcer tests, and roadmap/spec updates for the adapter-owned follow-up tasks.\n\nCo-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* Fix announcer preemption and repeat deduplication

Address review feedback by allowing assertive announcements to preempt an in-flight polite announcement, emitting the VoiceOver dedup marker on the first repeated message, and syncing the accessibility spec snippet and timing guidance with the new behavior.\n\nCo-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* Rust fmt

Fix the F6 menu hint getting cutt off by announcing the current world

commit.message:
feat(admin-ui): add provider and model brand icons

Add backend-owned icon identity resolution for providers and models, expose normalized icon keys through admin models and request log APIs, and render brand icons in the admin web UI using static Lobe icon assets.

- add /api/v1/admin/models with normalized provider/model icon metadata
- persist request log icon identity in metadata for historical stability
- support optional provider display.label/display.icon_key config
- render icons in models and request logs views
- document supported provider icon keys in config docs

commit.message:
feat(evolution): add governed improvement benchmark proof

Summary:
- add the governed_improvement benchmark module, richer benchmark-gate policy, and operator benchmark surfaces for anti-misevolution, canary, rollback, and receipt visibility
- expand runtime evals, backend tests, cockpit coverage, and shipped-truth docs for the governed improvement batch
- tighten the operator contract to report recent saved proposal receipts truthfully and degrade summary status when governed benchmark regressions exist

Rationale:
- Batch BM needs deterministic proof that self-improvement stays governed, review-gated, and rollback-ready rather than drifting silently
- the operator surface has to show only what the system can actually prove from persisted receipts, not blocked validations that were never saved

Tests:
- python3 -m py_compile backend/src/api/operator.py backend/src/evals/benchmark_catalog.py backend/src/evals/harness.py backend/src/evolution/engine.py backend/src/evolution/benchmark.py backend/tests/test_eval_harness.py backend/tests/test_evolution_api.py backend/tests/test_operator_api.py
- cd backend && .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_evolution_api.py tests/test_operator_api.py tests/test_eval_harness.py -q -o addopts=''
- cd frontend && NODE_OPTIONS=--experimental-require-module npx vitest run src/components/cockpit/CockpitView.test.tsx
- cd frontend && npm run build
- cd docs && npm run build
- git diff --check

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
